tactical_shape: Pushed on relentlessly down the sides with Alan Franco and Nilson Angulo
Counterattacking through midfield with Moisés Caicedo leading the intensity
Dropped back to defend a precious lead after scoring
standout_players: Gonzalo Plata — match-winner with the 77th-minute strike; prodded home the winner with the outside of his boot
Moisés Caicedo — imposing, led Ecuador’s intensity and ambition in midfield
Nilson Angulo — one of the new faces; scored the equaliser from the edge of the box
John Yeboah — at the heart of the action, weaved through the Germany midfield several times
Kevin Rodríguez — involved in the build-up to Plata’s winner and nearly scored earlier
Hernán Galíndez — largely untroubled; made sure Germany were kept at bay
home_read.momentum: A stirring win over Germany restores belief and sends Ecuador into the last 32, with the performance offering a potential lift for Beccacece’s scrutiny-hit side.

tactical_shape: Started with a full-strength side despite only injury-induced changes
Regained a grip after the hydration break but were otherwise largely frustrated
David Raum’s left-side deliveries were the main creative threat
standout_players: Florian Wirtz — set up the opener
Kai Havertz — had a header straight at Galíndez
Deniz Undav — top scorer but only introduced on the hour mark
fatigue_injuries: Julian Nagelsmann made only injury-induced changes to the starting XI
away_read.momentum: An error-prone, uninspired display ends with a defeat; Germany’s main narrative is frustration despite already having secured top spot in the group.
